---
title: "Performance Outliers: Deviation Bar Chart"
author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: "../1_data/babychecker.csv"
  
  # Variables
  entity_var: "Health Facilities"
  time_var: "year"
  filter_time: 2025
  value_var: "literacy_rate"
  
  # The Baseline Target (e.g., the District Average or a Policy Goal)
  baseline_target: 65
  
  # Policy-Facing Labels
  metric_name: "Standardized Literacy Rate"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.path = "../4_output/figures/",
  fig.width = 9,
  fig.height = 6, 
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)

# Load data
raw_data <- read_csv(params$data_path)

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# ==============================================================================
# Define string parameters OUTSIDE the mutate chain to prevent vector duplication
filter_str <- as.character(params$filter_time)

# Process data to calculate deviations from the baseline
plot_data <- raw_data %>%
  # 1. Safely drop NAs
  drop_na(!!sym(params$time_var), !!sym(params$entity_var), !!sym(params$value_var)) %>%
  
  # 2. Type-Safe Filtering for the specific time period
  mutate(Time_Raw = as.character(!!sym(params$time_var))) %>%
  filter(Time_Raw == filter_str) %>%
  
  # 3. Scrub and map
  mutate(
    Entity = str_wrap(str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(!!sym(params$entity_var)))), width = 15),
    Value = as.numeric(!!sym(params$value_var)),
    Target = as.numeric(params$baseline_target),
    
    # Calculate the gap
    Deviation = Value - Target,
    
    # Flag performance for coloring
    Performance = ifelse(Deviation >= 0, "Above Target", "Below Target")
  ) %>%
  # 4. Sort from highest over-performer to lowest under-performer
  arrange(Deviation) %>%
  mutate(Entity = factor(Entity, levels = unique(Entity)))
```

## Relative Performance Tracking

```{r plot-deviation}
source("../2_R/2.0_setup_theme.R", local = TRUE)

# Dynamically assign colors (Blue for positive, Red for negative)
perf_colors <- c("Above Target" = unname(cerp_cols["primary"]), 
                 "Below Target" = "#D7191C")

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Deviation, y = Entity, fill = Performance)) +
  
  # Draw the horizontal bars
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  
  # Add direct text labels on the bars showing the exact +/- gap
  geom_text(aes(
      label = ifelse(Deviation > 0, paste0("+", round(Deviation, 1)), round(Deviation, 1)),
      # Nudge the text slightly outside the bar based on the direction it is facing
      hjust = ifelse(Deviation > 0, -0.2, 1.2)
    ), 
    fontface = "bold", size = 3.5, color = cerp_cols["dark"]
  ) +
  
  # Draw the heavy anchor line at Zero (which represents the Baseline Target)
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = cerp_cols["dark"], linewidth = 1) +
  
  # Apply colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = perf_colors) +
  
  # Expand limits slightly so the text labels do not get cut off
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.15, 0.15))) +
  
  theme_cerp() +
  
  # Clean up the visual noise
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(), # Remove horizontal lines for clean bars
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),        # Hide the X-axis numbers since we use direct labels
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold")
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = paste("Outlier Analysis:", params$metric_name),
    subtitle = paste("Comparing regional performance against the baseline target of", params$baseline_target, "for", params$filter_time, "."),
    x = "Deviation from Target",
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
  )
```
